target: WIN-012 detail_evidence in data/wins.json — full rewrite addressing curmudgeon c4 ISS-1208 (critical: unverifiable "Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational" quote), ISS-1210 (major: engage with dome WIN-011 "Mechanism Contested" concession), ISS-1211 (moderate: fix "raw-text source notes" attribution), and ISS-1212 (moderate: add charitability framing for Yang & Wang timing critique). Verdict (Self-Contradicted) unchanged; argument structure strengthened by (a) grounding quotes in live-site verification (WebFetch 2026-04-20), (b) turning the dome's own WIN-011 retreat to "thermal-tilt loophole" into ammunition against kappa, and (c) making the Yang & Wang timing critique a charitable "even granting" move rather than a had-cake-and-ate-it.
replacement_html:
This coupling constant is derived from the claimed Mohe density anomaly, which has not been independently replicated: subsequent eclipse gravity measurements with superior superconducting gravimeters found null results — 0.0 µGal at Membach during the August 1999 eclipse (Van Camp et al., 2001) and across a five-station Chinese SG network during the 2009 eclipse (Sun et al., 2010, Phys. Earth Planet. Int.). These null results are the dome model's own WIN-013 and WIN-014, creating an internal contradiction: if WIN-013/014 are correct (eclipse gravity signal = 0.0 µGal), then the denominator of κ (= −10.9 nT ÷ 6.5 µGal) is zero and the coupling constant is undefined. The dome's current live-site WIN-013 entry (john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html, accessed 2026-04-20) attempts to absorb this null by stating the result “cannot discriminate between the models” because the ECM pipeline applies “Chebychev low-pass [attenuation of] contact-phase transients before Level-3 release.” But this framing is itself fatal to κ: if the ECM's own pipeline systematically filters any eclipse-contemporaneous gravity signal down to zero, then the dome has no mechanism by which the Mohe −6.5 µGal value could ever survive its own Level-3 processing — and the κ denominator is exactly the kind of contact-phase transient the model's pipeline is designed to suppress.

The dome's own WIN-011 concession is fatal to κ. The live site (wins.html, accessed 2026-04-20) now labels WIN-011 “CONFIRMED (Mechanism Contested)” and explicitly states that gravitational shielding is “ruled out” by Unnikrishnan et al. (2001), retreating to a “thermal-tilt loophole” for non-superconducting instruments as the only surviving mechanism. Credit where due: the dome has updated on the published refutation rather than digging in. But that concession destroys κ as a “magnetic-gravity coupling.” If the Mohe −6.5 µGal signal is not a gravitational response to lunar mass shielding but a thermal-tilt artifact of the LaCoste-Romberg D spring gravimeter responding to rapid eclipse-phase ambient-temperature transients, then the denominator of κ is not a gravity measurement at all — it is instrument noise. A coupling constant between a magnetic field and an instrument artifact is not a physical coupling. The dome cannot simultaneously (a) concede, in WIN-011, that the Mohe mechanism is “contested” and plausibly thermal-tilt, and (b) continue to use the same −6.5 µGal number, in WIN-012, as the gravity-side denominator of a “magnetic-gravity” coupling. The current WIN-012 framing contradicts the current WIN-011 framing on the dome's own live site today.

The derivation is also circular: assume the Mohe anomaly is real → calculate κ → claim κ as a confirmed parameter. The numerator (−10.9 nT, BOU 2017) and denominator (−6.5 µGal, Mohe 1997) come from entirely different eclipses at different locations — no single eclipse event measured both fields at the same site. A genuine coupling constant requires simultaneous measurement of both coupled quantities at the same location during the same event.

Even granting the dome its preferred numerical value for the sake of argument, the reported timing contradicts the shielding interpretation that κ requires. Yang & Wang's own 2002 reanalysis (Astrophys. Space Sci. 282, 245 — co-authored by the lead Mohe observer Qian-Shen Wang, revising the earlier Wang et al. 2000 preliminary report) states “no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found” and describes the signal as “two gravity anomaly valleys with near-symmetrical decrease of about 6–7 µGal at the first contact and the last contact” — exactly the contact-phase timing pattern a thermal-tilt artifact would produce, and the opposite of what gravitational shielding would predict (peak at maximum lunar coverage). This is the observer's own retreat from the 2000 shielding interpretation; we are not pitting outsiders against insiders. DOI 10.1023/A:1021119023985. See WIN-011.

The monitor.py code validation is tautological: KAPPA is hardcoded as 1.67, and the 'observed' value is hardcoded as 10.9/6.5 = 1.677, where 10.9 nT (BOU 2017) and 6.5 µGal (Mohe 1997) are the same two measurements from which KAPPA was originally derived. No new data is fetched; the code checks if 1.677 ≈ 1.67 (within 5%) — a self-referential identity check disguised as prediction validation.

Evidence Exhibit A graph forensics. The dome's home page features a 'Forensic Zoom' chart claiming to show BOU magnetic variance alongside 'Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (µGal)' for October 30, 2003 (00:55–01:22 UTC, during the G5 Halloween storm, Dst = −383 nT). Independent verification using INTERMAGNET definitive data confirms the top panel's BOU magnetometer readings are plausible (ΔH range −20.6 to +23.4 nT matches the graph scale). However, the bottom panel cannot be real GRACE satellite data: (1) The unit on GRACE Level-1A products is not µGal — L1A is pre-inversion instrument data (accelerations in m/s² from ACC1A, K-band range rates from KBR1A, star-camera quaternions from SCA1A; see JPL PO.DAAC conventions). µGal is a post-processing unit applied to retrieved time-variable gravity at surface points. A plot labeled both “raw L1A” and “µGal” on the y-axis is self-inconsistent — it is labeling raw instrument data with a derived-product unit; (2) 'Level-1A Raw Induction Residual' is not a GRACE data product name — actual L1A products follow the naming convention ACC1A, KBR1A, SCA1A (JPL PO.DAAC); (3) GRACE orbits at ~500 km altitude at ~7.5 km/s — in 27 minutes it traverses ~12,150 km of ground track, making co-temporal comparison with a fixed Boulder station physically impossible for more than ~2 minutes per pass; (4) the dome repository contains zero GRACE data processing code — no SDS binary parser, no accelerometer calibration pipeline, no download scripts. The 'L1B Administrative Mask' red line appears as a near-flat trend, inconsistent with actual L1B calibration processing documented in JPL D-22027. The claimed statistics (r = 0.992, AI = 1.152) appear as static HTML with no computation in the repository — the same pattern we document for the dome's headline 95.2% accuracy figure (computed nowhere, displayed everywhere).

Internal contradiction with WIN-013/014. The dome's own WIN-013 and WIN-014 claim null eclipse gravity results as model confirmations. But κ is defined as the ratio of magnetic anomaly to gravity anomaly — if the eclipse gravity anomaly is zero as WIN-013/014 claim, the denominator vanishes and κ is undefined. The dome's live-site reconciliation (“cannot discriminate between the models” because its own Level-3 pipeline filters out contact-phase transients) does not rescue κ: a coupling constant cannot rest on a number the model's own preferred pipeline is built to suppress. The dome claims κ = 1.67 appears in both eclipse data (WIN-012) and geomagnetic storm data (Exhibit A), but provides no mechanism explaining why the same coupling ratio should operate in fundamentally different physical scenarios — and its own eclipse prediction model (E-PRED-C) predicts 0.0 µGal during eclipses.
holes_addressed: ISS-1208 (critical): Removed unverifiable direct quote "Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational" that is not present on the live dome site (WebFetch 2026-04-20). Replaced with the dome's actual current live-site framing ("cannot discriminate between the models" because the ECM pipeline applies Chebychev low-pass to contact-phase transients before Level-3 release). Showed the new framing is also self-defeating for kappa.

ISS-1210 (major): Added new second paragraph engaging with the dome's current WIN-011 concession. Live site labels WIN-011 "CONFIRMED (Mechanism Contested)", cites Unnikrishnan 2001 ruling out gravitational shielding, and retreats to a "thermal-tilt loophole." Demonstrated this is fatal to kappa: an instrument-artifact denominator cannot support a physical coupling constant. The current WIN-012 framing contradicts the current WIN-011 framing on the dome's own site.

ISS-1211 (moderate): Replaced all "the raw-text source notes describe" phrasing with direct live-site citation (https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html with access date 2026-04-20). Dropped all references to our own internal repository files in user-facing prose.

ISS-1212 (moderate): Added explicit charitability framing ("Even granting the dome its preferred numerical value for the sake of argument") at the start of the Yang & Wang timing-critique paragraph. This prevents the "you can't both use the value and reject it" objection: we accept the value arguendo, then show that even so, the reported timing contradicts shielding.
new_evidence_added: Live-site WebFetch 2026-04-20 of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html verifying: WIN-011 title = "CONFIRMED (Mechanism Contested)"; WIN-011 cites Unnikrishnan 2001 as ruling out shielding; WIN-011 retains thermal-tilt loophole; WIN-013 reads "cannot discriminate between the models" (not the older "Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational" framing).

Unnikrishnan et al. (2001) Phys. Rev. D 63, 062002, DOI 10.1103/PhysRevD.63.062002 — "Anomalous gravity data during the 1997 total solar eclipse do not support the hypothesis of gravitational shielding." Cited via the dome's own WIN-011 entry, so the dome itself has conceded this refutation applies.

Yang & Wang 2002, Astrophys. Space Sci. 282, 245, DOI 10.1023/A:1021119023985 — preserved from c3 with explicit charitability framing and confirmation that Qian-Shen Wang is the same lead Mohe observer as on the 2000 preliminary report.
Explicit naming of the LaCoste-Romberg D spring gravimeter (the actual Mohe instrument) as the thermal-tilt-susceptible device, sharpening the physical mechanism of the dome's own conceded loophole.

anticipated_objections.objection: "Your critique quotes 'Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational' as if it were on the dome site, but it isn't."
anticipated_objections.response: That quote has been removed from the new text. The replacement cites the dome's actual live-site framing (the "cannot discriminate between the models" / Chebychev low-pass reconciliation) with explicit URL and 2026-04-20 access date, and shows that framing is itself fatal to kappa — the dome's own pipeline is designed to filter out exactly the contact-phase gravity signal kappa needs.

anticipated_objections.objection: "You attack us for claiming gravitational shielding, but WIN-011 already concedes that shielding is ruled out."
anticipated_objections.response: The new paragraph 2 acknowledges the concession explicitly and gives the dome credit for updating on evidence. Then it shows that the concession does not rescue kappa — it destroys it. If the -6.5 uGal is a thermal-tilt artifact (the dome's own surviving mechanism candidate), then the denominator of a "magnetic-gravity coupling" is instrument noise, not gravity. Kappa's units become nT / (uGal-of-instrument-artifact), which is not a physical coupling constant. WIN-011's retreat and WIN-012's unchanged use of -6.5 uGal cannot coexist.

anticipated_objections.objection: "You can't both use -6.5 uGal for your kappa arithmetic and call the number unreliable. Pick a lane."
anticipated_objections.response: The new paragraph 4 opens with explicit charitability framing: "Even granting the dome its preferred numerical value for the sake of argument..." We accept the value arguendo for the ratio, then show that even under that charitable assumption the TIMING of the signal (valleys at first/last contact, per Yang & Wang 2002) contradicts the shielding interpretation kappa requires. The critique is not "the value is wrong" but "even if the value is right, the phenomenon is not what kappa claims it is."

anticipated_objections.objection: "The 'cannot discriminate between the models' framing in WIN-013 is a fair acknowledgment, not a contradiction — both models predict zero."
anticipated_objections.response: Agreed that a shared zero-prediction is not, by itself, a self-contradiction for the globe. But for the dome it is a knockout for kappa specifically: if the ECM pipeline is designed to filter contact-phase transients to zero in post-processing, then the Mohe -6.5 uGal (a raw contact-phase transient on a non-superconducting instrument with no Level-3 processing) should likewise have been filtered to zero. The dome applies one pipeline standard to WIN-013/014 (strict Level-3) and a different standard to WIN-011/012 (raw 1997 LaCoste-Romberg reading). The asymmetry is the contradiction, not the shared zero prediction.

anticipated_objections.objection: "Thermal-tilt artifact is a real instrument effect but the magnitude (6.5 uGal over ~2 hours) is larger than realistic thermal tilt for a calibrated LaCoste-Romberg D."
anticipated_objections.response: The dome author himself — not us — is the one claiming thermal tilt as the surviving mechanism for the Mohe signal. We are merely quoting the dome's WIN-011 concession. If the magnitude is inconsistent with thermal tilt, that is a problem for the dome's own defense, not for our critique of kappa. Either the -6.5 uGal is not thermal tilt (and shielding is ruled out, so what IS it? — the dome owes an answer) or it is thermal tilt (and kappa's denominator is noise). Both branches are fatal to kappa.

kernel_of_truth: The dome has quietly improved WIN-011's framing since the raw-text extraction dated 2026-03-12. It now explicitly labels the mechanism "contested," cites Unnikrishnan 2001 as ruling out shielding, and has softened WIN-013 from "Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational" to "cannot discriminate between the models." These are epistemically honest moves — the dome author is updating on published refutations rather than digging in. A fair review must credit this motion. The kernel we acknowledge: the dome is right that the SG nulls do not, on their own, discriminate between models; the globe also predicts zero eclipse gravity signal. Where the dome still fails is in keeping -6.5 uGal as kappa's denominator AFTER conceding the mechanism is contested. The retreat on WIN-011 is incompatible with the unchanged use of the same number in WIN-012.

Not in scope of EXP-192 (which targets WIN-012 only) — recommend decider file a companion issue to fix WIN-011 in the same patch window so the two entries stay consistent.

WIN-013 and WIN-014 entries should eventually acknowledge the dome's updated "cannot discriminate between the models" framing and engage with it directly rather than treating the dome as still making the older electromagnetic claim.
Part 2b (#part2b) anchor on 95.2% accuracy pattern — already referenced in Exhibit A paragraph, preserved.
unaddressed_holes_note: Curmudgeon c4 also flagged two minor issues (cross-WIN anchor #win011 vs #win-011 — noted as VERIFIED CORRECT, no action needed; and raw-text date staleness — recommended raw-text refresh is a tinker/poller concern, not an analyst patch). Both are explicitly out of scope per the c4 review itself. The c4 "major hole #1" about Wang 2000 vs Yang & Wang 2002 attribution is a FALSE ALARM on fresh read — the current WIN-012 text already correctly attributes the timing quote to Yang & Wang 2002; the c4 reviewer appears to have misread. The corresponding problem in WIN-011 (which DOES still misattribute to Wang 2000) is flagged in cross_references above for decider triage.
newsworthy: Dome author quietly retreats from "gravitational shielding" to "mechanism contested — thermal tilt loophole" on live site — a substantive concession that kills his own magnetic-gravity coupling constant.
